lesson: Initialize repo-local lesson stores before the pre-merge audit because the standalone hlscreen repo previously only had MEMORY.md.

Use all-features fmt, clippy, tests, debug build, release build, diff check, read-only scan, and fixture/runtime smokes before opening the hlscreen PR; cargo-audit and cargo-deny are not installed in this environment.

The audit found hidden contract drift that green tests missed: feature windows must be timestamp-bounded, health status must only escalate by severity, doctor must fail closed on invalid config, and API percent decoding must preserve UTF-8 and return 400 on malformed input.

For public-ready hlscreen packaging, include MIT license, contribution/security/support/conduct docs, GitHub CI/templates/dependabot, release/privacy/threat-model/roadmap docs, deterministic SVG screenshots, and a truthful MSRV; the current lockfile requires Rust 1.88, not 1.85.

For hlscreen live public WebSocket mode, reconnect/resubscribe must be runtime behavior, not only a simulated health model; live recording must use a bounded writer worker and fail closed on backpressure, and live normalized events must preserve receive timestamps for latency/debug evidence.

For hlscreen screenshots, regenerate from current CLI output after rebuilding hls-cli, preserve SVG whitespace so table alignment survives browser rendering, redact temp paths, and visually preview SVGs with rsvg-convert before publishing.

A professional hlscreen TUI can stay deterministic and CI-friendly by computing truthful KPI rows from FeatureSnapshot data, keeping ANSI out of stable stdout, and using generated SVG plus PNG preview as the visual gate before PR.

For hlscreen v2, implement the Spec Kit task file in order: T001-T019 are foundation contracts only, so keep confidence computation, replay parity, why-ranked UI, resilience, metadata, metrics output, and extension execution for later story slices.
commands: cargo test -p hls-core --test confidence_state --test score_breakdown --test metrics_contract

lesson: For hlscreen UI polish, keep the stable renderer deterministic and ANSI-free, make rows scan through truthful FeatureSnapshot-derived KPIs and selected-symbol detail, regenerate SVG screenshots from the rebuilt binary, and rerun full-pipeline smoke tests because layout copy changes can break integration assertions.

Confidence should be attached to FeatureSnapshot before UI polish, so live/replay/screen/TUI surfaces share the same data-quality evidence; do not fake confidence in the renderer. After this slice, replay parity persistence and --verify-parity remain open.

US1 replay parity is now implemented: confidence baselines are stored in SQLite confidence_snapshots, hls replay --verify-parity writes the first baseline and fails non-zero on drift, and live/replay stdout includes confidence_summary for scriptable checks.

US2 liquidity resilience works best as shared FeatureSnapshot data, not renderer-only copy: keep BBO history bounded, label BBO OFI and adverse-selection as top-of-book proxies, emit unknown for insufficient quote history, then expose the same fields through screen presets and TUI screenshots.

Why-ranked UI should consume a shared ScoreBreakdown attached to FeatureSnapshot, not renderer-only strings; expose the same components through CLI JSON, screen DSL score_component.<name>, and deterministic screenshots so replay, TUI, and filters stay in parity.

Metadata-backed TUI polish should attach optional public MetadataEnrichment to FeatureSnapshot rows and expose the same tags through screen presets, SQLite cache, and deterministic screenshots; missing tokenDetails fields must become unknown_metadata, not ingestion failures or renderer-only guesses.

US5 operations should stay contract-first: benchmark packs hash canonical public fixture outputs, doctor metrics reject high-cardinality labels, extension manifests validate read-only/no-permission boundaries without executing WASM, and release packaging remains tag-gated until a reviewed v* workflow proves it.
